INTRODUCTION
The Division of Capital Asset Management & Maintenance (DCAMM) is pleased to publish the
Commonwealth of Massachusetts Real Property Report for 2025. The Report includes Commonwealth
property, both owned and leased. In addition to this PDF version of the report, DCAMM is also introducing
a fully searchable online version of the report this year available on DCAMM’s website.
This report has been prepared in accordance with the requirements of M.G.L Chapter 7C, Section 39. The
information contained herein was compiled using the Commonwealth Asset Management Information
System (“CAMIS”), an enterprise-wide platform overseen by the DCAMM Office of Finance and
Administration to track the Commonwealth’s land and building portfolio, as well as DCAMM’s centralized
leasing database. The information is continuously updated, and the reports include information current
as of April 2025.
REPORT ORGANIZATION
This PDF version of the report contains five tables providing different ways of organizing and displaying
information about property owned and leased by the Commonwealth:
TABLE 1: SUMMARY OF COMMONWEALTH-OWNED REAL PROPERTY BY EXECUTIVE OFFICE
TABLE 2: SUMMARY OF COMMONWEALTH-OWNED REAL PROPERTY BY COUNTY
TABLE 3: COMMONWEALTH-OWNED REAL PROPERTY BY EXECUTIVE OFFICE AND AGENCY
TABLE 4: BUILDINGS, IMPROVEMENTS AND LAND AT STATE FACILITIES BY MUNICIPALITY
TABLE 5: COMMONWEALTH ACTIVE LEASE AGREEMENTS BY SECRETARIAT AND MUNICIPALITY
Each table provides a range of details about the properties, such as site name, site acreage, building
name, building square footage and site improvements. The specific details provided in each table differ
slightly depending upon how the table is sorted and grouped. Buildings are categorized as either major
or minor buildings. Site improvements are categorized as either horizontal (i.e. a parking lot), vertical
(i.e. a fire tower) or linear (i.e. a walking trail).
Appendix I provides a summary of Commonwealth-owned real property by executive office excluding
Massachusetts State College Building Authority (MSCBA), UMass Building Authority (UMBA), Higher Ed
Assistance Corporation (HEAC) and Foundations as well as other entity-owned buildings.
Appendix II lists county-owned land and buildings that are occupied by various Sheriffs and Trial Court
Departments. These buildings and associated land are not included in any other tables in the report.
Appendix III lists property with conservation restrictions and agricultural preservation restrictions plus
certain easements. Legal ownership of these lands remains with a municipal or private party. The
Commonwealth only owns restrictions on certain “development rights” tied to the property, not the
property itself.
1

Appendix IV describes the data sources used to prepare this report.
Appendix V is a glossary of terms providing an explanation of agency codes as well as other abbreviations
used in this report. This Appendix also defines technical terms used in collecting data and in generating
the report.
Appendix VI is a municipality-county index to look up which county a municipality is located in.
DATA ON AUTHORITY-OWNED PROPERTY
For higher education campuses, Tables 1-4 include information available for land and buildings owned by
MSCBA, UMBA, HEAC and Foundations. However, each respective entity should be contacted directly for
the definitive current inventory. In Table 4, MSCBA, UMBA, HEAC and Foundation-owned buildings are
listed with their campus and italicized for easy identification.
Assets owned by other authorities and independent agencies are included in this report only if the
Commonwealth uses or occupies those facilities. Assets owned by authorities such as MWRA, MBTA, and
Massport and other independent agencies are not included in this report except in rare cases when assets
are used or occupied by the Commonwealth. These entities should be contacted directly for information
about their holdings.
ONGOING DATA QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS
The annual Real Property Report presents a snapshot of a dynamic land and building portfolio. DCAMM
continues with systematic quality review and technology enhancements for CAMIS reporting, including
implementing an online searchable version of the report.
For comments or questions about this report or any Commonwealth-owned property, please contact
the Division of Capital Asset Management & Maintenance, Office of Finance and Administration, One
Ashburton Place, Boston, MA 02108 or at info.DCAMM@mass.gov.
2

Appendix IV
Data Sources
387

Appendix IV: DATA SOURCES
In the late 1980's, the Massachusetts Asset Management System (MAssets) staff collected data from all 351
municipalities across the Commonwealth and from state agencies reporting their land and building holdings. In
1999 pursuant to legislative authorization, DCAMM initiated the Capital Asset Management Information System
(CAMIS). Through this program DCAMM conducted a comprehensive condition assessment of the state’s capital
assets and procured software to manage the collected data. In 2015 the MAssets data was merged into the
CAMIS database.
Now, through its office of Finance and Administration, DCAMM has implemented the CAMIS program to both assist
facilities in their daily maintenance activities and to provide data for DCAMM staff to optimize statewide capital
maintenance and repair planning, and to track state land and building assets. CAMIS staff consult with facilities' staff
throughout the state to update information about their buildings and improvements. DCAMM also consults with
assessors of cities and towns statewide to verify information about parcels in their municipalities, and with various
state agencies to confirm their land holdings and update any change in status.
DCAMM’s Office of Real Estate Management (OREM) manages the purchase and sale of real property for the
majority of Commonwealth agencies. OREM works with the CAMIS team and DCAMM’s Office of the General Counsel
to ensure that each acquisition and disposition of land and buildings is logged into the Commonwealth portfolio of
real property. They also report on telecom leases on state property, easements, land swaps, and care & control
agreements between agencies.
The Office of Leasing maintains information on properties leased on behalf of Commonwealth agencies. The
information contained in the Leasing database is constantly changing, based on the commencement and expiration,
as well as the terms, of each agreement. The Leasing Office diligently seeks the most appropriate and inexpensive
rental space available to suit each agency’s needs and records information on each agreement in their database as
each transaction is completed.
The information in the Real Property database is dynamic. This RPR publication is therefore a comprehensive annual
snapshot of the existing portfolio. Due to the tremendous volume of land and building data gathered over decades,
DCAMM and its agency partners are constantly reviewing CAMIS data to ensure accuracy and making changes to the
data when warranted.
Reporting on the Commonwealth’s Real Property is in accordance with the requirements of M.G.L. Chapter 7C,
Section 39.
388
